At the 2025 FINRA Annual Conference, experts from regulatory bodies and financial firms said AI is […]

data.title: Japan intends to reach tariff agreement with the United States before the June G7 summit
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Japanese Economy Minister Ryomasa Akasawa said his goal is to resolve tariff negotiations in time for a meeting between U.S. President Trump and Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba in June. "It has been confirmed that the leaders of both countries are looking forward to their meeting," Akasawa told reporters after meeting with U.S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick and U.S. Trade Representative Greer in Washington on Friday. "We will keep this in mind and do our best." Before he made the above remarks, Trump and Ishiba had a phone call and promised to meet during the G7 summit next month. A few hours later, Trump said he agreed to a partnership between Nippon Steel and U.S. Steel (X.N). (Jin Shi)

data.title: Gate Launchpool launches MIRAI (MIRAI), stake and share 1,014,198 MIRAI rewards
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Gate Launchpool is about to open MIRAI (MIRAI), the event time is May 26 20:00 - June 2 20:00 (UTC+8), users who pledge USDT and AVAAI to participate in Launchpool can share 1,014,198 MIRAI tokens for free. Among them, the USDT new user mining pool is expected to reach an annualized rate of 95.08%, and the MIRAI mining pool is expected to reach an annualized rate of 380.68%. It is now open for early staking.

data.title: The New York Times released a preliminary list of Trump dinner attendees
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to statistics from the New York Times reporter on the scene, and confirmed through on-site photos, the people who participated in the TRUMP dinner include: Justin Sun, Washington lawyer Elliot Berke, Wintermute founder and CEO Evgeny Gaevoy, two co-founders of Delphi Digital Anil Lulla and Yan Liberman, Chinese cryptocurrency investor Cheng Lu, CTS International founder Stephen Dworkin, digital asset management agency Hyperithm CEO Sangrok Oh, conservative media person Caitlin Sinclair, former NBA star Lamar Odom, Acheron Trading founder Wesley Pryor, NFT platform Magic Eden co-founder and CEO Jack Tan Lu, two executives of hedge fund Hyper-Alpha Capital Asher Ang and Lean Sheng Tan, Synthetix founder Kain Warwick, Singapore engineer Joel Lee, Kronos Research CEO Vincent Liu, Wexford Capital partner Paul Jacobi, etc.

data.title: Whale Activity: Significant AAVE Purchase Follows WBTC Sale
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on-chain data analyst Yu Jin has reported notable trading activity involving a large-scale investor or institution. After selling 184.4 WBTC, valued at approximately $20.4 million, through Wintermute OTC yesterday, the entity has now purchased $15 million worth of AAVE through the same platform.

The transaction involved transferring $15 million USDC to Wintermute, resulting in the acquisition of 57,715 AAVE tokens at a price of $259.9 each. Since March 2023, the investor has been accumulating AAVE, spending a total of $30.8 million to amass 224,010 AAVE tokens at an average price of $137.5. This strategy has yielded an unrealized profit of $26.32 million.

data.title: AAVE hoarding whales/institutions bought another $15 million worth of AAVE today
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain data analyst Yu Jin, the whale/institution that sold 184.4 WBTC (about 20.4 million US dollars) through Wintermute OTC yesterday bought 15 million US dollars worth of AAVE through Wintermute today.
They transferred 15 million USDC to Wintermute, and then received 57,715 AAVE from Wintermute, which means the purchase price is 259.9 US dollars.
They have been hoarding AAVE since March 2023. At present, they have spent a total of 30.8 million US dollars to hoard 224,010 AAVE, with an average price of 137.5 US dollars, and currently have a floating profit of 26.32 million US dollars.

data.title: The Cyberspace Administration of China has dealt with illegal online financial accounts in accordance with the law, including multiple cryptocurrency-related accounts
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Recently, the Cyberspace Administration of China, together with the financial management department, has dealt with a number of accounts and websites that spread false information about the capital market, made illegal stock recommendations, and speculated on virtual currency transactions in accordance with the law.
Some typical cases are reported as follows:
1. Accounts such as "Love Stocks APP" published false information about the capital market.
2. Accounts such as "Kan Ge Talks about Finance" made illegal stock recommendations.
3. Accounts and websites such as "Huo Ge Chats about Cryptocurrency" promoted and speculated on virtual currency transactions.
4. Accounts such as "Xiao Beiyou" spread information about black and gray industries in the financial field. (Cyberspace China)

data.title: Polygon faces ‘Big L’ as co-founder Mihailo Bjelic steps down
data.description:
Mihailo Bjelic, co-founder of Ethereum layer-2 scaling solution Polygon, has stepped down from his role at Polygon but suggests he will stay active in the crypto industry in some capacity. His resignation drew reactions across Polygon and the wider crypto community, with several seeing it as a significant loss for Polygon, which has been tied to several major developments in recent months. Bjelic winds down ‘day-to-day involvement’“After much thought and reflection, I’ve decided to step down from the board of the Polygon Foundation and wind down my day-to-day involvement with Polygon Labs,” Bjelic said in a May 23 X post. “I’ll always be cheering from the sidelines and supporting however and whenever I can,” Bjelic added
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/polygon-cofounder-mihailo-bjelic-steps-down-operations?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
